goal: Replace the simulator's MODELED NVLink tier with a MEASURED intra-node NCCL all-reduce bus bandwidth, then re-fit island_tax/node_tax.

harness.onPodBenchmark: tools/bench_nccl_allreduce.py (torchrun, one rank per GPU; emits busbw per message size)
harness.orchestrator: tools/runpod_nccl_bench.py (reuses tools/runpod_rlvr.py pod lifecycle: create -> poll SSH -> rsync -> run -> copy report -> ALWAYS delete)
harness.unitTested: tests/test_cluster_netcalib.py (command builder, busbw inversion, measured-provenance tagging, sim wiring)

environmentProbe.gpuStock: NVIDIA H100 80GB HBM3 (SXM) secureCloud stockStatus=High, available=true

whyNotRun: The RunPod MCP can create a pod, but it exposes no pod-exec / pod-logs / file-read tool, so running torchrun on the pod and retrieving the JSON report requires SSH. This container has no ssh client and no RUNPOD_API_KEY in the shell, so the SSH-based orchestrator cannot run. Creating a paid pod whose results cannot be collected would waste money and risk orphaning a billable resource, so no pod was created.

currentCalibration.note: Derived from documented interconnect line rates (NVLink 400 / NVSwitch 120 / RoCE 50 GB/s), exposed-comm-fraction 0.15. Upper-bound, worst-case comm-bound model.

unblockNeeds: A host with an ssh client + RUNPOD_API_KEY (e.g. a local machine or a runner with SSH egress to ssh.runpod.io), OR
A RunPod MCP pod-exec/log-retrieval capability so the benchmark can run and return its report without SSH.
